health_zone_table.basis: SitRep46 Table 2 (29 June 2026) visually reviewed against the INSP PDF and reconciled to Table 1 / province totals 1333/399. No new affected health zone was reported this cycle; the affected-zone count holds at 35/104. The table carries 17 Ituri cases in zones not yet identified; these remain an unventilated residual and are not distributed to named health zones. Wamba (Haut-Uele) imported cases are counted at Nia-Nia and returned to Nia-Nia. PDF sha256 6b36553a0203f198c8b6808621182a73d06bf92cdadda60b0a0052f9d7c77beb.

reconciliation.note: Rows promoted here reconcile to official province/national totals: 1316 named confirmed cases plus 17 Ituri cases awaiting ventilation = 1333; all 399 confirmed deaths are named-zone attributed. The residual row is retained as unventilated context and excluded from mappable named-zone attribution.

lab_indicators_24h.note: National aggregate from SitRep46 section 4.3: Ituri 64 collected/analyzed / 17 positive; Nord-Kivu 97 received / 77 analyzed / 9 positive; Sud-Kivu 0 collected/analyzed. Positivity uses 26 positives over 141 analyzed samples. The Nord-Kivu challenge table still flags a sample backlog and delayed diagnosis.

care_continuity: End-of-day isolation stood at 609 patients (216 confirmed, 393 suspected) at 92.7% global occupancy.
Ituri isolation occupancy was 102% with 451 patients isolated; Nord-Kivu was 125% with 135; Sud-Kivu 51.1% with 23.
Exits included 18 deaths, 9 recoveries and 33 non-cases; 5 escapes were recorded (3 Ituri, 2 Nord-Kivu).
highlights: 26 new confirmed cases including 8 confirmed deaths were reported for 2026-06-29, in Ituri (17 including 4 deaths) and Nord-Kivu (9 including 4 deaths).
New confirmed cases were distributed across Bunia (14), Nizi (2) and Lita (1) in Ituri, and Katwa (7), Butembo (1) and Musienene (1) in Nord-Kivu.

14 additional deaths were recorded among previously confirmed CTE patients (13 in Ituri: 4 CTE Elikya, 4 CTE CME, 2 CTE Mongbwalu, 2 CTE Nyankunde, 1 CTE ISTM; 1 in Nord-Kivu CTE Beni), bringing confirmed deaths to 399.
No new affected health zone was reported on 2026-06-29; the affected-zone count holds at 35/104 across the three provinces.

9 patients were declared recovered after negative control tests (6 in Ituri, 3 in Nord-Kivu), bringing DRC cumulative recoveries to 189 (176 Ituri, 11 Nord-Kivu, 2 Sud-Kivu). Free care began in four Ituri priority zones: Bunia, Mongbwalu, Nyankunde, Rwampara.
laboratory: SitRep46 reports Ituri collected and analyzed 64 samples with 17 positive (26.6%).
Nord-Kivu received 97 samples and analyzed 77, with 9 positive (11.7%). Sud-Kivu collected and analyzed no samples.
publication_context: INSP SitRep 46 reports through 2026-06-29 and was published on the INSP WordPress site (post 25070; PDF media 25071; WP publish 2026-07-01T06:42:54Z). The PDF states a publication date of 30 June 2026.

No SitRep 45 (report date 2026-06-28) is visible in the INSP posts/media API; a DRC Ministry of Communication and Media bulletin reported 1,307 confirmed / 377 deaths for 2026-06-28 but is not an INSP SitRep and is not promoted. SitRep46 supersedes SitRep44 directly, so cumulative deltas span two report-days (28 and 29 June).
The outbreak is reported across 3 provinces and 35 of 104 health zones; no new affected zone this SitRep.
Ituri remains the epicenter with 1214 of 1333 DRC confirmed cases (91.1%).
Nord-Kivu holds the highest provincial confirmed-case fatality ratio at 54.3%, led by Oicha (66.7%), Kyondo (66.7%), Beni (63.6%) and Katwa (60.0%); 100% lethality observed in low-count Vuhovi.
redactions: Page contact details and operational names, if present outside table facts, are intentionally not promoted into public evidence text.
surveillance: 1,413 alerts were reported and 1,078 investigated, producing a 76.3% investigation rate; Ituri 35.2% (180/511), Nord-Kivu 99.8% (862/864), Sud-Kivu 94.7% (36/38).
309 suspected cases of the day were identified, including 90 deceased validated alerts.
11,796 contacts were under follow-up and 9,756 were seen, producing 82.7% follow-up coverage (completeness 93.3%; Kalunguta and Kilo had no data at consolidation).

988 new contacts were listed (872 across 11 Ituri health zones and 116 across 4 Nord-Kivu health zones); 262 contacts completed 21-day follow-up (173 in Ituri, 89 in Nord-Kivu). All Sud-Kivu contacts exited follow-up.

alerts_total.data_gap_note: Report (J-1) 37; new alerts 1376; total alerts of the day 1413. A narrative sentence alternately cites 1,341 alerts reported / 1,047 investigated (75.9%) with 87 deceased validated alerts, which conflicts with the Table 3 totals promoted here (1,413 / 1,078 / 76.3%, 90 deceased). 988 new contacts listed (872 in 11 Ituri HZs, 116 in 4 Nord-Kivu HZs); 262 completed 21-day follow-up (173 Ituri, 89 Nord-Kivu).

challenges.finding: Community reluctance toward post-mortem sampling (EDS/swabs) and resistance delay confirmation, underestimate cases, and disrupt key activities.
challenges.response: Intensify RCCE, involve religious and community leaders, and reinforce team security.

challenges.finding: Insufficient CTE capacity with Nord-Kivu saturation cited at 138.8% in the challenge table (Table 6 reports Nord-Kivu occupancy 125% and national 92.7% this cycle), raising nosocomial-transmission risk.
challenges.response: Accelerate CTE construction/expansion and deploy additional temporary isolation structures.

challenges.finding: Contact follow-up performance reached 82.7% (challenge table cites 82.5%) versus the 95% target, leaving transmission chains uninterrupted.
challenges.response: Train, deploy, and motivate community relays (RECO) with reinforced supervision.

challenges.finding: Weak alert reporting in some affected health zones risks undetected, silent transmission.
challenges.response: Reinforce community-based surveillance and rapid alert investigation.

challenges.finding: Seventeen Ituri confirmed cases remain not yet ventilated by health zone, leaving gaps in transmission-chain reconstruction.
challenges.response: Conduct data reconciliation, prune duplicates, and reclassify/localize with active field verification.

challenges.finding: Insufficient supply of essential medicines (MEG) and medical inputs weakens holistic case management.
challenges.response: Advocacy and urgent procurement of essential medicines.

challenges.finding: Insufficient IPC inputs (PPE, chlorine) and limited training raise nosocomial-infection risk; the challenge table reports 96 health-worker infections and 19 deaths (CFR 19.7%).
challenges.response: Urgent procurement and reinforced IPC training.

challenges.finding: A backlog of un-analyzed samples in Nord-Kivu and late diagnosis persist amid a 54.2% provincial CFR (challenge table).
challenges.response: Deploy a laboratory catch-up plan and reinforce diagnostic capacity.

challenges.finding: Insufficient ambulances and isolation structures (a gap of about 20 centers) delay transfer and isolation and raise transmission risk.
challenges.response: Urgent deployment of logistics and additional infrastructure.

challenges.finding: Insufficient coordination and weak use of COUSP frameworks cause operational inefficiency and delayed decisions.
challenges.response: Reinforce coordination mechanisms at all levels and operationalize provincial COUSP.

challenges.finding: A financial-resource gap of about USD 20 million limits implementation across all response pillars.
challenges.response: Urgent resource mobilization and partner advocacy.

challenges.finding: Insecurity and limited access (CODECO, ADF) restrict operations and access to high-risk zones.
challenges.response: Strengthen security coordination and humanitarian access.

challenges.finding: Compromised continuity of essential (non-Ebola) health services raises indirect mortality.
challenges.response: Effectively implement free care and reinforce continuity of essential health services (CEHS).

challenges.finding: High population mobility raises the risk of rapid geographic expansion.
challenges.response: Reinforce PoE/PoC surveillance and cross-border coordination.

contacts_total.data_gap_note: Follow-up rate computed only for reporting ZS (completeness 93.3%); Kalunguta (Nord-Kivu) and Kilo (Ituri) had no data at consolidation. All Sud-Kivu contacts exited follow-up this cycle. 988 new contacts listed (872 in 11 Ituri HZs, 116 in 4 Nord-Kivu HZs); 262 completed 21-day follow-up (173 Ituri, 89 Nord-Kivu).

priorities.priority: Resolve the 17 unventilated Ituri confirmed cases without distributing them to named health zones
Relieve Nord-Kivu isolation saturation and Ituri load (102% occupancy); close the reported gap of about 20 isolation centers
Improve contact follow-up (82.7% vs 95% target) and clear the Nord-Kivu laboratory backlog given the 54.3% provincial CFR

preserved_conflicts: SitRep46 headline is DRC-only (1333 confirmed / 399 confirmed deaths across Ituri, Nord-Kivu, Sud-Kivu); the country-scope public snapshot adds the latest reviewed Uganda component separately.

The 1,333 confirmed total is marked re-actualized after DHIS2 cleaning/harmonization; SitRep45 (28 June) was not published by INSP, so treat the +59 confirmed and +39 confirmed deaths vs SitRep44 as official revised cumulative deltas spanning two report-days (28 and 29 June), not single-day incident-line-list deltas.

No SitRep 45 is visible in the INSP posts/media API; a DRC Ministry of Communication and Media bulletin dated 28 June (1,307 confirmed / 377 deaths) is provisional government-communications data and is not promoted into scored counts.
SitRep46 carries an explicit Ituri residual of 17 confirmed cases in zones not yet identified; these counts are public but not mappable to named health zones.
No new affected health zone was reported on 2026-06-29; the affected-zone count holds at 35/104 (SitRep44 had added Mandima).

The Faits saillants sentence reads 'Dix-Sept (26)' new confirmed cases; this is a wording artifact (Ituri contributed 17 of the 26), and Table 1 confirms 26 new confirmed cases (Ituri 17, Nord-Kivu 9) with 8 deaths.
The banner case-fatality label reads 29,7% while Table 1 and Table 2 report 29,9%; 399/1333 = 29.9% is used.
Daily suspected cases (309) and the 90 deceased validated alerts are current-cycle validated alert outputs, not cumulative suspected stock or cumulative confirmed deaths.

Surveillance figures carry an internal inconsistency: Table 3 totals 1,413 alerts reported / 1,078 investigated (76.3%) with 90 deceased validated alerts, while a narrative sentence cites 1,341 reported / 1,047 investigated (75.9%) with 87 deceased; the Table 3 values are promoted.

The isolation table is internally inconsistent: the Ituri row reports 451 patients isolated but its confirmed (200) + suspected (254) sub-cells sum to 454, so the national suspected-in-isolation sub-count as printed (396) exceeds census-minus-confirmed by 3. The authoritative end-of-day census (609) and confirmed sub-count (216) are retained, and suspected-in-isolation is reconciled to the residual 393 (216 + 393 = 609; Ituri suspects 251).

A Nia-Nia footnote (Table 2) records that imported cases from Wamba ZS (Haut-Uele) are counted at Nia-Nia and returned to Nia-Nia; this is an Ituri-linked cross-zone attribution, not a Haut-Uele denominator addition, and the affected-province count remains 3.

Uganda anchor remains 20 cumulative confirmed cases (2 deaths, 11 recoveries) per the Uganda MoH EVD daily dashboard (last reviewed 2026-06-21); carried forward this cycle as INSP reports DRC only, so country-scope is 1353 confirmed / 401 deaths / 200 recovered.

Sud-Kivu has recorded no new confirmed case since 2026-05-26, suggesting no recent transmission; it remains at 3 confirmed / 1 death across Miti-Murhesa, and all Sud-Kivu contacts exited follow-up this cycle.

review_notes: SitRep #046 (report date 2026-06-29; INSP WordPress post 25070 published 2026-07-01T06:42:54Z; embedded PDF media 25071). PDF sha256 6b36553a0203f198c8b6808621182a73d06bf92cdadda60b0a0052f9d7c77beb, 13 pages. Title N046/MVB_29/06/2026; the PDF states a publication date of 30 June 2026; treated as report-date 2026-06-29.

No SitRep 45 (28 June) is visible in the INSP posts/media API; a DRC Ministry of Communication and Media bulletin reported 1,307/377 for 28 June but is provisional government communications, not promoted. SitRep46 supersedes SitRep44 directly, so cumulative deltas span two report-days.

DRC headline 1333 confirmed / 399 confirmed deaths (CFR 29.9%; the banner label 29,7% is a typo); +59 confirmed and +39 confirmed deaths vs SitRep44. On 29 June, 26 new confirmed (Ituri 17, Nord-Kivu 9) carried 8 deaths; a further 14 deaths occurred among previously confirmed CTE patients (13 Ituri, 1 Nord-Kivu).
The 1,333 total is flagged re-actualized after DHIS2 cleaning/harmonization; deltas are official revised deltas.
No new affected health zone was reported; the affected-zone count holds at 35 of 104; Ituri now 23/36 touched. No new map geometry is required this cycle.
Health-zone rows reconcile to 1333 confirmed / 399 deaths: 1316 named confirmed cases plus 17 Ituri cases awaiting ventilation, with all 399 confirmed deaths named-zone attributed.

Recovered advanced to 189 DRC cumulative (176 Ituri / 11 Nord-Kivu / 2 Sud-Kivu). Uganda anchor carried forward at 20 cumulative cases / 2 deaths / 11 recoveries (last reviewed 2026-06-21), so country-scope is 1353 confirmed / 401 deaths / 200 recovered.

Laboratory throughput promoted from section 4.3: Ituri 64 collected/analyzed, 17 positive; Nord-Kivu 97 received / 77 analyzed, 9 positive; Sud-Kivu 0 (141 analyzed / 26 positive overall, 18.4%). The Nord-Kivu backlog and delayed diagnosis remain a flagged challenge.

Operational data captured: Table 3 alerts 1,413 reported / 1,078 investigated (76.3%; a narrative sentence conflicts at 1,341/1,047/75.9%), 309 suspected cases of the day (90 deceased validated alerts), 11,796 contacts under follow-up (82.7%), 609 in isolation (216 confirmed / 396 suspected, 92.7% occupancy), 988 new contacts, PoC activation 68/69 with 159,385 travelers passed. A PoC under construction at Miala (north Bunia) was burned overnight on 29 June.
Nia-Nia footnote retained: imported Wamba ZS (Haut-Uele) cases are counted at Nia-Nia and returned to Nia-Nia; recorded as an Ituri-linked cross-zone attribution only, not a fourth affected province.
